page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture and is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The instance does not include integrated graphics processing units or local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage.

Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, this instance relies on network-attached resources for persistent storage operations. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other tenants. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a dense memory footprint suitable for workloads that require substantial memory capacity without the added cost of scaling up the virtual core count.

The n4a-highmem-8 instance is designed for memory-intensive applications that do not require local disk performance or hardware acceleration. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks where the working dataset fits within the 64.0 GB memory limit.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-8 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4a server family, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable performance for memory-bound workloads. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for applications requiring significant memory capacity without the need for high compute core counts. It is suitable for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
